notes: SHIP v1.14.239 — MASTER_U_TABLE SEEDED FROM VENDOR SPEC (data-only follow-on to .238; John directed the web lookup). .238 shipped the honesty machinery: an unlisted model is UNKNOWN and gets flagged gold rather than given an invented span. That left the real DFW02 master at 38.8% unknown (907/2338) — too much gold to device-verify against, and the 'a clean rack renders unflagged' checklist item could not have passed. This ship seeds the table with heights CONFIRMED FROM VENDOR DOCUMENTATION, each row carrying its source in a comment. NEW ROWS: (1) NVIDIA Quantum-2 QM9700/QM9790 = 1U — NVIDIA datasheet, '64 ports of NDR 400Gb/s InfiniBand per port in a 1U standard chassis' (528 hosts on DFW02; the old 1U guess happened to be RIGHT). (2) NVIDIA Spectrum SN2201 = 1U — NVIDIA datasheet, '1U open Ethernet switch', 43.9mm height (240 hosts; old guess RIGHT). (3) Supermicro AS-2125HS-TNR = 2U — Supermicro product page, '2U Hyper A+ Server' (50 hosts; ⚠ the old default was drawing it 1U — WRONG). (4) Dell PowerEdge R760 = 2U — Dell spec sheet, H 86.8mm (36 hosts; ⚠ old default drew it 1U — WRONG). (5) Dell PowerEdge R660 = 1U — Dell, '1U, two-socket' (1 host; old guess RIGHT). RESULT on the real DFW02 master: unknown drops 38.8% → 2.2%; resolved 61.2% → 97.8%; 0 mismatches vs vendor spec; guard test confirms no new pattern swallows a GPU node, an NVL tray, or an in-name NRU model. ⚠️ DELIBERATELY NOT SEEDED — these stay FLAGGED until John makes a field call, because guessing them is precisely the sin .238 exists to kill: (a) VAST DBox (50 hosts on DFW02) — VAST ships the enclosure as BOTH a 1U (Ceres) and a 2U (Lightspeed DF-5630) and the master string 'VAST DBox' does not disambiguate which is on the floor; (b) SN5600 — appears only in the synthetic phantom-stress fixture, never yet in a real master; (c) the literal model 'X' (2 hosts) — garbage data, correctly stays unknown. ⚠️ BEHAVIOR CHANGE, INTENTIONAL: master_nodeHeightU() now returns 2 (was 1) for AS-2125 and R760. This is a CORRECTION toward truth, not a regression, but it is NOT display-only — it changes the U-span those devices occupy in master_rackToElevation, which feeds Rack Map, Master search, AND the mscope seeder that persists slots into deployment records. Consequences John should expect: (i) newly-created deployments seed those devices with correct 2U spans; deployments created BEFORE .239 keep their old 1U spans (not retroactively re-derived — a later master reload does not rewrite an existing deployment); (ii) preflight_run may now surface NEW U-collisions for AS-2125/R760 that it could not see before, because a 2U chassis overlaps a neighbour a 1U guess did not. Those are REAL collisions that were being hidden by the undersized guess — surfacing them is the point. Gates: node --check ×4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS, CSS braces 3856/3856 balanced, CRLF preserved, three-stamp .238→.239, table verified against the exact DFW02 model strings. ?legacy=1 unaffected (data-only change to a shared helper whose consumers are unchanged). DEVICE-VERIFY: fold into the .238 pass (batch = 2). On DFW02 the aisle should now be mostly UNFLAGGED — expect gold only on the 50 VAST DBox hosts and the 2 'X' rows. OPEN FOR JOHN: which VAST enclosure is on the floor — 1U Ceres or 2U Lightspeed? One word seeds the last row and takes DFW02 to 99.9%.
